sanskrit:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव।
येन भूतान्यशेषाणि द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि॥
transliteration:
yaj jïätvä na punar moham evaà yäsyasi päëòava
yena bhütäny açeñäëi drakñyasy ätmany atho mayi
literal_meaning: Having known which, you shall not again fall into this illusion, O Pāṇḍava; by which you will see all beings without exception in the Self and then in Me.
contextual_meaning: True knowledge dissolves the illusion of separateness; once this insight is internalized, one perceives every living being as an expression of the same Ground of Being, ending existential confusion and relational conflict.
psychological_state: Post-insight integration: the mind that once fragmented reality into 'me vs. them' now reflexively sees self-same awareness animating all forms, removing the cognitive root of anxiety and moral fatigue.
trigger_scenario: A burnout tech-lead, after a guided psilocybin session, suddenly intuits that the colleague he resents is not separate from him; the grudge dissolves and performance anxiety drops.
anchor_text: See every being as your own Self, and rest in the One.
